key_requirements: React + Vite locked — sub-stack choices are within this constraint
Complex 5-state mastery algorithm requires deterministic, testable state transitions
localStorage primary + Redis backup — state persistence is load-bearing
Offline-first PWA — service worker must cache everything on install
Atomic build tasks — every implementation step completable by a less-capable model

note: React + Vite and Upstash + Vercel are locked by constraint CON-001 and CON-003. These approaches evaluate the sub-stack choices within that constraint: state management library, TypeScript vs JavaScript, PWA tooling (service worker setup), and tab routing pattern. These are the genuine open decisions for this project.

approaches.name: JavaScript + Context/useReducer + vite-plugin-pwa
approaches.summary: Plain JavaScript with React Context and useReducer for state management. vite-plugin-pwa handles the service worker and PWA manifest. Tab routing is managed as state in the root Context. All question states stored in a single localStorage JSON blob under one key. Zero external state management dependencies.

technology_stack.routing: Tab index as Context state (no router library)
additional: uuid npm package for device UUID generation

research_sources: React official docs: useReducer for complex state (https://react.dev/reference/react/useReducer)
MDN: Service Worker API cache-first strategy
strengths: Zero extra dependencies beyond vite-plugin-pwa — no state library adds no bundle weight
useReducer is ideal for a finite state machine pattern — dispatch(action) maps naturally to state transitions
Context + useReducer is the React team's own recommendation for app-level state without external dependencies
vite-plugin-pwa is the de facto standard for Vite PWA setup — handles iOS Safari manifest quirks automatically
Single localStorage key design is simple and easy to understand in atomic build tasks
weaknesses: Context + useReducer requires more boilerplate per component than Zustand — each atomic task includes more setup code for a less-capable model
No TypeScript means type errors in the 5-state algorithm surface only at runtime — a subtle streak-tracking bug could go undetected
Manual localStorage serialization/deserialization on every state change adds custom code that must be tested
React Context re-renders all consumers on every state change — in a 131-question state tree, performance profiling may be needed
No devtools integration — debugging the algorithm state requires console.log or custom tooling
risk_factors: Algorithm correctness risk: without TypeScript, the 5-state machine's transition logic has no compile-time guard
Re-render performance: Context updates are not selective — all consumers re-render, which may cause jank during session question rendering
localStorage schema versioning requires manual migration code
approaches.scalability_assessment: Single user, no scale concern. Architecture is adequate for the project's lifetime.

approaches.name: TypeScript + Zustand + vite-plugin-pwa
approaches.summary: TypeScript for the full application with Zustand as the state management library. Zustand's persist middleware handles localStorage synchronization automatically. vite-plugin-pwa generates the service worker and manifest. Tab routing is a Zustand store field. The 5-state mastery algorithm is fully typed with interfaces for QuestionState, SessionRecord, and MasteryTransition.

technology_stack.routing: Tab index as Zustand store field (no router library)
technology_stack.persistence: Zustand persist middleware (localStorage key: fap_store) + custom Redis sync layer

research_sources: TypeScript handbook: discriminated unions for state machines
Zustand GitHub: 5.7k+ production apps, React team recommended alternative to Redux
strengths: TypeScript interfaces catch algorithm bugs at compile time — streak counters, reversal arrays, and session tracking are fully typed
Zustand persist middleware eliminates all custom localStorage serialization code — reduces implementation surface by ~200 lines
Zustand's selector pattern prevents unnecessary re-renders — each component subscribes only to the slice it needs
Atomic build tasks are more precise with TypeScript: every task can specify exact interface fields, reducing ambiguity for a less-capable model
Zustand devtools makes algorithm state visible during development without custom tooling
vite-plugin-pwa handles iOS Safari 'Add to Home Screen' flow and Android install prompt edge cases automatically
Discriminated union types for QuestionState (New|Active|NearMastery|Mastered|Maintenance) make exhaustive case handling compiler-enforced
weaknesses: TypeScript compilation step adds build complexity — tsconfig.json must be correctly configured for Vite + React
Zustand persist middleware has opinions about store shape — migrating the schema if requirements change requires a version migration function
Adds ~15KB to bundle (Zustand is small but not zero) compared to Context/useReducer
A less-capable model must understand Zustand store patterns — slightly higher conceptual overhead than pure React primitives
risk_factors: Zustand persist migration: if localStorage schema needs to change post-deployment, migration functions must be implemented correctly
TypeScript tsconfig strictness level affects how much type safety is enforced — must be configured before first build task
approaches.scalability_assessment: Single user. Zustand scales well beyond this project's needs — no scale concern.

approaches.name: TypeScript + Zustand + Custom Service Worker
approaches.summary: TypeScript with Zustand for state management, but the PWA service worker is written manually rather than generated by vite-plugin-pwa. The custom service worker gives full control over cache strategy, cache naming, and update behavior. Same state and persistence approach as Approach B, but PWA tooling is hand-rolled.

technology_stack.pwa_tooling: Custom service worker (sw.ts compiled via Vite worker build) + vite-plugin-legacy for manifest
technology_stack.routing: Tab index as Zustand store field
technology_stack.persistence: Zustand persist middleware + custom Redis sync layer

research_sources: MDN: Service Worker API — install, activate, fetch lifecycle
web.dev: Workbox vs custom service workers (https://web.dev/articles/service-workers-cache-storage)
PWABuilder documentation: manual service worker patterns
strengths: Full control over cache strategy — can implement exact cache behavior without Workbox abstraction
No dependency on vite-plugin-pwa — one fewer plugin in the Vite build chain
Cache version string is explicit in code — cache invalidation behavior is fully transparent
weaknesses: Service worker development is notoriously tricky — iOS Safari has specific gotchas around update behavior and cache scope that Workbox handles automatically
A custom service worker requires manual asset manifest generation — must enumerate build output filenames to cache, or use glob patterns that could miss files
Vite content hashes in filenames change every build — custom SW must regenerate the asset list on each build, requiring a post-build script or Vite plugin
Significantly more code to write and maintain for the same offline result that vite-plugin-pwa delivers in 10 lines of config
iOS Safari PWA has a list of known quirks (navigation preload, update handling) that Workbox already patches — custom SW must solve these independently
risk_factors: High risk: iOS Safari service worker behavior is the most common source of PWA install failure — custom SW is more likely to hit unhandled edge cases
Maintenance burden: any Vite upgrade that changes output filenames requires updating the cache manifest generation script
Service worker update lifecycle bugs can silently serve stale content after deployment
approaches.scalability_assessment: Not a scalability concern — service worker behavior is unrelated to scale.

preliminary_recommendation.rationale: Approach B (TypeScript + Zustand + vite-plugin-pwa) best serves the two most demanding constraints: atomic build tasks and algorithm correctness. TypeScript makes every atomic task unambiguous — a less-capable model receives typed interfaces, not implicit conventions. Zustand persist middleware eliminates a class of custom localStorage code that would otherwise need its own atomic tasks and tests. vite-plugin-pwa is the community standard for Vite PWA setup and handles iOS Safari edge cases that a custom service worker would need to solve independently. The bundle overhead (~15KB for Zustand) is negligible for a PWA that will be cached on device. Approach A is viable but riskier for the algorithm. Approach C adds complexity with no offsetting benefit.
